scope: Experiment registry, search-facing test guardrails, monitoring thresholds, incident runbook, and Phase D closeout.
evidence_unavailable: Google Business Profile owner diagnostics and local action metrics
Finance margin, refund, VAT, fee, CAC, and contribution definitions
Owner-approved status definitions for booking accepted/completed/cancelled/no-show and order fulfilled/refunded/returned

no_launch_until: D-1 KPI tree, event dictionary, consent behavior, and key-event policy are approved.
D-2 reconciliation rules and owner-approved statuses are available.
D-3 platform readback is available for Search Console, GBP, Merchant Center, and analytics as applicable.
D-4 critical journey accessibility warnings are fixed or explicitly outside the tested path.
Every test has a primary metric, guardrails, sample plan, stop rules, decision rule, owner, and rollback path.
search_controls: Variants must preserve stable self-canonicals unless the test explicitly concerns canonical architecture and has SEO owner approval.
Variants must not create unstable redirect, noindex, robots, sitemap, hreflang, or schema behavior.
Search-facing page tests must have release annotations, tested URL groups, and rollback criteria.
Do not use search snippets, schema, feed facts, or local facts that differ from visible page truth.
quality_controls: Accessibility, privacy, payment safety, booking/order reconciliation, stock/price truth, and local fact accuracy outrank conversion lift.
A click, booking start, add-to-cart, checkout-success view, contact click, or analytics key event is not revenue or fulfilled customer value by itself.
Before/after comparisons are observational unless exposure, timing, demand, seasonality, inventory, campaign, and tracking confounders are controlled.

monitoring_thresholds.signal: Production robots.txt, sitemap.xml, product sitemaps, and merchant feed return expected status and content type.
monitoring_thresholds.threshold: Blocker if robots.txt is 404 or a sitemap/feed URL serves HTML instead of XML/text for any launch check.
monitoring_thresholds.cadence: per release and daily until fixed after launch
monitoring_thresholds.source: public HTTP fetch and Search Console sitemap readback

monitoring_thresholds.signal: Manual actions, security issues, indexing, sitemap, enhancement, page experience, and performance segments.
monitoring_thresholds.threshold: Blocker for manual action/security issue; critical for sudden indexable URL collapse/spike, sitemap errors, template-wide enhancement errors, or unexplained organic click/impression anomaly after data-delay window.
monitoring_thresholds.cadence: weekly prelaunch, then monthly plus release and incident checks

monitoring_thresholds.signal: Feed fetch success, item disapprovals, price/availability mismatch, destination status, and product URL errors.
monitoring_thresholds.threshold: Blocker if feed cannot fetch or public feed URL is not XML; critical if price/availability mismatch or disapproval affects a material product set.
monitoring_thresholds.cadence: daily during feed launch, then weekly
monitoring_thresholds.source: Merchant Center diagnostics and public feed fetch

monitoring_thresholds.signal: Ownership, NAP, categories, hours/special hours, website URL, booking URL, reviews, calls, directions, and duplicate/suspension issues.
monitoring_thresholds.threshold: Critical for owner loss, suspension, wrong NAP/hours/category, website URL mismatch, or unresolved duplicate; warning for review-response SLA breach.
monitoring_thresholds.cadence: weekly during launch cleanup, then monthly plus special-hours changes

monitoring_thresholds.signal: Approved events fire once at the correct state, with correct consent state, route, language, page type, and no prohibited parameters.
monitoring_thresholds.threshold: Blocker for prohibited PII, firing outside approved consent, duplicate key event, or key event fired without server source; critical for missing primary journey events after implementation.
monitoring_thresholds.cadence: per release, then weekly dashboard QA
monitoring_thresholds.source: analytics debug, event export, source review, and server reconciliation

monitoring_thresholds.signal: Analytics booking/order/payment events reconcile to bookings, orders, payment_events, invoice events, and operational statuses.
monitoring_thresholds.threshold: Blocker if purchase is inferred from checkout-success page view; critical if daily key-event to server-record discrepancy exceeds owner-approved tolerance or payment webhook mismatch appears.
monitoring_thresholds.source: Supabase/business exports and approved analytics platform

monitoring_thresholds.signal: Booking, checkout, contact, product, account, and recovery journeys remain keyboard, screen-reader, mobile, and browser-agent operable.
monitoring_thresholds.threshold: Blocker for inaccessible booking/checkout/contact completion; critical for duplicate high-impact controls, missing labels, missing field-associated errors, or focus traps.
monitoring_thresholds.cadence: per release and before any conversion experiment
monitoring_thresholds.source: browser smoke, accessibility tree, keyboard review, and issue tracker

monitoring_thresholds.signal: Field CWV and lab diagnostics for representative homepage, service, product, contact, cart, and checkout routes.
monitoring_thresholds.threshold: Critical if field data or lab checks show a material regression on primary journeys after release; warning for non-critical lab regressions with no field data.
monitoring_thresholds.cadence: per release for lab, monthly for field where available

release_annotations: content, metadata, schema, sitemap, redirect, feed, checkout, booking, analytics, consent, or accessibility changes
experiment id and variant policy when applicable
known exceptions, owners, rollback condition, and next readback date

incident_runbook.action: Verify data integrity first: tracking, consent, platform delays, filters, timezones, bot/internal traffic, and source-system exports.
Scope the issue by route type, language, page group, device, market, platform, source, funnel stage, and date/time.
Check production access, status, robots, noindex, canonical, redirects, sitemap/feed, schema, rendered content, and major deployment changes.
Check Search Console, Merchant Center, GBP, analytics, CrUX/PageSpeed, payment/order/booking systems, and known platform incidents.
Separate technical regression, tracking defect, inventory/capacity change, demand/seasonality, market/SERP change, and content/trust issue before assigning cause.
Apply the smallest reversible fix, verify through the affected source, annotate the incident, update the board, and add a regression check.

primary_blockers: Platform readback unavailable for Search Console, GBP, Merchant Center, analytics, CrUX/PageSpeed, and live business systems.
Production robots/sitemap/product sitemap/merchant feed mismatch remains unresolved from D-3.
Owner-approved business, finance, booking/order status, and privacy/analytics policies are not approved.
D-4 checkout accessibility warnings remain open.
phase_d_gate_result.next_required_phase: Phase E - Release Closeout, Evidence Ledger, And Growth Handoff
